parentQuestion: An infant born after prolonged labor with the arm pulled forcefully during delivery presents with a 'claw hand' — hyperextension at the MCP joints and flexion at the IP joints of the 4th and 5th digits. Which nerve roots are most likely injured?

scaffolds.q: The hand has intrinsic muscles that control fine finger movements.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Intrinsic hand muscles (inside the hand itself) control delicate movements like spreading fingers, fine grip, and balancing finger position.

scaffolds.q: The brachial plexus has lower roots (C8-T1) that mainly control the hand.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! C8-T1 (lower brachial plexus) give rise to nerves that control intrinsic hand muscles. Upper roots (C5-C6) control shoulder and elbow.

scaffolds.q: The ulnar nerve controls most of the small muscles inside the hand.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The ulnar nerve (from C8-T1) supplies most intrinsic hand muscles - the interossei, lumbricals for 4th/5th fingers, hypothenar muscles.

scaffolds.q: A 'claw hand' deformity involves fingers that are bent in an abnormal position.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! In claw hand, the MCP joints are hyperextended (bent backward) while the IP joints are flexed (curled). The fingers look like a claw.

scaffolds.q: Claw hand happens when intrinsic hand muscles are paralyzed.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Intrinsic muscles normally flex the MCP joints and extend the IP joints. Without them, the extrinsic flexors (in the forearm) overpower and curl the fingers into a claw.

scaffolds.q: The 4th and 5th fingers are most affected in ulnar nerve injury.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The ulnar nerve mainly supplies the 4th and 5th fingers. Claw deformity is most prominent in these fingers when the ulnar nerve is damaged.

scaffolds.q: Lower brachial plexus injury (C8-T1) is called Klumpke's palsy.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Klumpke's palsy = lower plexus injury (C8-T1). It typically occurs when the arm is pulled forcefully upward during delivery. The classic sign is claw hand.

scaffolds.q: Klumpke's palsy causes claw hand because C8-T1 roots supply the intrinsic hand muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! C8-T1 give rise to the ulnar nerve and medial contribution to median nerve. These control intrinsic hand muscles. Damage causes claw hand deformity.

scaffolds.q: An infant with claw hand deformity after difficult delivery most likely has C8-T1 injury (Klumpke's palsy), not C5-C6 injury.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Claw hand = lower plexus (C8-T1 = Klumpke's). Waiter's tip = upper plexus (C5-C6 = Erb-Duchenne). The presentation tells you which roots are injured.
